description:
A small folder with three raw profiling logs is already present in your home directory:

/home/user/raw_runs/app_alpha.run  
/home/user/raw_runs/app_beta.run  
/home/user/raw_runs/app_gamma.run  

Each “.run” file contains one ASCII line per profiling tick, e.g.

tick 1  
tick 2  
…  

As a performance-engineer you want to archive these logs and create a tiny post-processing report.

Your job

1. Make a new workspace hierarchy under /home/user/profiler/ containing two sub-directories named results and summary.
2. Move (or copy) the *.run files from /home/user/raw_runs/ into /home/user/profiler/results/.  Keep the original filenames.
3. Count how many lines each file contains.  This number equals the collected sample count.
4. Produce a single report file called /home/user/profiler/summary/run_summary.log with **exactly** the following structure so the grader can parse it:

    • The first line is an ASCII header spelled exactly:  
      AppName<TAB>Samples  

    • One subsequent line per application, ordered alphabetically by filename (app_alpha first, then app_beta, then app_gamma).  
      Each data line has: the basename of the “.run” file (without extension), a single horizontal TAB character, and the integer line-count. Example placeholder:  
      app_alpha<TAB>15  

    • After the three data lines, append a final line that starts with “Generated on: ” followed by a single space and a timestamp created with the command:  
      date "+%F %T"  

      Example pattern (your actual timestamp will differ):  
      Generated on: 2025-03-14 16:05:23  

    • The file must end with a single trailing newline (Unix LF).

5. Leave the source directory /home/user/raw_runs/ otherwise untouched.

Hints  
• Use wc -l or similar to obtain line counts.  
• Remember to insert real TAB characters, not spaces, between the two columns.  
• You can generate the timestamp in one command substitution when you create/append the file.  

The automated test will check:  
• Existence of the two new directories.  
• Presence of the three .run files inside /home/user/profiler/results/.  
• Exact byte-for-byte contents of /home/user/profiler/summary/run_summary.log (except the date, which is validated by a regex).  
• That the source files in /home/user/raw_runs/ are still present.  

No root privileges are needed.
truth:
Existing files before the task starts
-------------------------------------
1. Directory: /home/user/raw_runs/
2. Files and contents (Unix LF line endings):

/home/user/raw_runs/app_alpha.run
tick 1
tick 2
tick 3
tick 4
tick 5
tick 6
tick 7
tick 8
tick 9
tick 10
tick 11
tick 12
tick 13
tick 14
tick 15
<EOF>

/home/user/raw_runs/app_beta.run
tick 1
tick 2
tick 3
tick 4
tick 5
tick 6
tick 7
tick 8
tick 9
tick 10
tick 11
tick 12
tick 13
tick 14
tick 15
tick 16
tick 17
tick 18
tick 19
tick 20
tick 21
tick 22
<EOF>

/home/user/raw_runs/app_gamma.run
tick 1
tick 2
tick 3
tick 4
tick 5
tick 6
tick 7
tick 8
tick 9
<EOF>

Expected artefacts after successful completion
----------------------------------------------
1. Directory tree created:

/home/user/profiler/
/home/user/profiler/results/
/home/user/profiler/summary/

2. Three .run files located in /home/user/profiler/results/ with *identical* contents to their counterparts in /home/user/raw_runs/.

3. File: /home/user/profiler/summary/run_summary.log  
   Contents must match the exact pattern:

AppName<TAB>Samples\n
app_alpha<TAB>15\n
app_beta<TAB>22\n
app_gamma<TAB>9\n
Generated on: YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS\n

Constraints for the final line:
• YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS must be the output of `date "+%F %T"` executed on the container during the agent’s run.
• The timestamp will be checked by the regex: ^Generated on: [0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2} [0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}$

4. The original directory /home/user/raw_runs/ and the original three files remain untouched.

Permissions
-----------
• All new directories: 755  
• All copied/moved files: 644  

The grading script will fail if:
• Tabs are replaced with spaces in run_summary.log.
• Filenames are mis-ordered.
• The header or “Generated on:” line differs in spelling/case.
